key_risks: Hawkish FOMC dots or Powell commentary on 4.2% headline CPI could hit high-beta momentum names
Sharp reversal of crowded semiconductor/Korea trade after large recent gains
Gold spike fading if geopolitical stress de-escalates, dragging the hedge negative
Weak May retail sales (6/17) hurting small-cap value
Factor rotation away from momentum into laggards

portfolio.rationale: Strongest momentum in the universe (+8.8% 7d, +75% 6m) with AI capex tailwinds intact; trend continuation favored into a likely benign FOMC hold.
Explosive momentum (+12.7% 7d) driven by semiconductor export cycle; high-beta extension of the chip trade.
Diversified exposure to leading factor with consistent trend persistence (+5.9% 7d, +30% 6m).
Cooling core CPI, falling yields, and strong domestic data support small-cap value breadth rally (+4.2% 7d).
Sharp +3.7% single-day spike into the close suggests geopolitical or macro hedging demand; serves as portfolio hedge against FOMC/geopolitical shocks.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward semiconductor/momentum leadership and rate-sensitive small-cap value into an expected FOMC hold with cooling core inflation and falling yields, with a gold position hedging against the apparent geopolitical stress signaled by the spike in gold/silver and drop in oil.
rationale_summary: Momentum continuation in semis, Korea, and momentum factor plus small-value breadth, hedged with gold given safe-haven flows and FOMC event risk.
